In the 1980s and 1990s, Portuguese public television had a very well-known commentator called Gabriel Alves, famous for his unusual and often unintentionally funny phrases while commentating football matches. His voice became strongly linked in my memory to the World Cups of my childhood, as well as European football throughout the 90s. Nowadays he has mostly disappeared from television, but he hasn’t been forgotten, and every now and then people share lists of his most memorable lines. Here are some of them:

“Low shot at mid-height over the bar!”

Juste Fontaine scored 13 goals in 6 games in the 1958 World Cup for France. Nobody has come close to scoring that many goals since then. It's been out of reach for decades.

But now with the expanded size of the cup, the inclusion of lower quality teams and an additional knock-out round, it's doable.

English football had been professional since 1885, and by the mid-1920s Austria, Hungary, Italy and the US had salaried players who were ineligible for the Olympics, so the case for a separate Fifa competition was clear. With its postwar Olympic wins and the year 1930 marking the country’s centenary, Uruguay was the obvious choice as host, but the competition’s beginnings were friable. Rimet organised a French side, but the coach and some players declined to travel.

just read in the Athletic an interesting quirk in the change of format for this World Cup, with head to heads replacing GD as the tiebreaker in the groups, but GD being the tiebreaker when it comes to deciding which third placed teams qualify for the knockouts. So in theory you could have a third placed team in a group not qualifying as one of the best third placed teams, because they have bad GD, whereas the 4th placed team in that group might actually have much better GD that would've qualified them.

Hot Take; this World Cup has shown there is enough talent for an expanded field without the quality suffering. I’d much rather them just bite the bullet and go to 64, having the third place teams qualify has removed a lot of the tension of the group stage.

1986 was Peak Maradona.

 

OTD, 22 June 1986, in the same World Cup quarter final, in four minutes Maradona scored the Hand of God (an illegal handball the ref allowed) and then the Goal of the Century (that run past basically the whole England team). Argentina won 2-1 and went on to win it all.

Haiti/Scotland's draw in this world cup is absolutely brutal. They got one of the toughest groups with Brazil and Morocco. If they finish 3rd they are projected to get Germany in the third round. If they finish 2nd they would get Netherlands. And as of now the winners get Japan.

Some bizarre scheduling at this World Cup. Group B finishes first on June 24th but the winner doesn’t play again until July 2nd, with 8 days rest. Yet, Group I’s winner, who finish on the 26th, has to play on June 30th, with only 4 days rest. Doesn’t seem the most fair.
